subject: Science
question_text: The muscular tube through which stored urine is passed out of the body is called –
answer_key: urethra
answer_explanation: Urethra is the tube that conducts urine from the urinary bladder to the outside of the body.

subject: Science
question_text: They are pipe-like, consisting of a group of specialised cells. They transport substances and form a two-way traffic in plants. Which of the following terms qualify for the features mentioned above?
answer_key: Phloem tissue
answer_explanation: Phloem is a vascular tissue that transports food synthesized in the leaves to all parts of the plant in a two-way traffic.

subject: Science
question_text: The absorption of nutrients and exchange of respiratory gases between blood and tissues takes place in –
answer_key: capillaries
answer_explanation: Capillaries are extremely thin-walled blood vessels where the actual exchange of gases, nutrients, and waste products occurs between blood and tissues.

subject: Science
question_text: In which of the following parts of human body are sweat glands absent?
answer_key: Lips
answer_explanation: Lips do not have sweat glands, which is why they do not sweat and can dry out easily.

subject: Science
question_text: In a tall tree, which force is responsible for pulling water and minerals from the soil?
answer_key: Suction force
answer_explanation: Transpiration from the leaves creates a suction pull, which pulls water from the roots to great heights in tall trees.
provenance_excerpt: In a tall tree, which force is responsible for pulling water and minerals from the soil?

answer_key: Ammonia

subject: Science
question_text: Veins have valves which allow blood to flow only in one direction. Arteries do not have valves. Yet the blood flows in one direction only. Can you explain why?
answer_key: Blood in arteries flows in one direction and under high pressure because it is being forcefully pumped directly by the heart.
answer_explanation: Understanding arterial blood flow.

question_text: What is the special feature present in a human heart which does not allow mixing of blood when oxygen-rich and carbon dioxide-rich blood reach the heart?
answer_key: The human heart is divided into four chambers with partitions (septum) between them that prevent the mixing of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood.
answer_explanation: Anatomy of the heart.

subject: Science
question_text:
Arrange the following statements in the correct order in which they occur during the formation and removal of urine in human beings.
(a) Ureters carry urine to the urinary bladder.
(b) Wastes dissolved in water is filtered out as urine in the kidneys.
(c) Urine stored in urinary bladder is passed out through the urinary opening at the end of the urethra.
(d) Blood containing useful and harmful substances reaches the kidneys for filtration.
(e) Useful substances are absorbed back into the blood.
answer_explanation: Ordering physiological processes.

subject: Science
question_text: Paheli uprooted a rose plant from the soil. Most of the root tips, with root hairs got left behind in the soil. She planted it in a pot with new soil and watered it regularly. Will the plant grow or die? Give reason for your answer.
answer_key: The plant might die. Root hairs are essential for the absorption of water and minerals from the soil. Without them, the plant cannot absorb enough water to survive.
answer_explanation: Applying root anatomy to plant survival.

subject: Science
question_text:
(a) Name the only artery that carries carbon dioxide-rich blood.
(b) Why is it called an artery if it does not carry oxygen-rich blood?
answer_key:
(a) Pulmonary artery.
(b) It is called an artery because it carries blood away from the heart (to the lungs), which is the defining characteristic of all arteries.
answer_explanation: Exception to standard blood vessel rules.

subject: Science
question_text:
Boojho’s uncle was hospitalised and put on dialysis after a severe infection in both of his kidneys.
(a) What is dialysis?
(b) When does it become necessary to take such a treatment?
answer_key:
(a) Dialysis is the artificial method of filtering the blood to remove waste products when the kidneys fail.
(b) It becomes necessary when a person suffers from kidney failure and their kidneys can no longer filter waste naturally.
answer_explanation: Understanding dialysis.

subject: Science
question_text:
Name the process and the organ which helps in removing the following wastes from the body.
(a) Carbondioxide
(b) Undigested food
(c) Urine
(d) Sweat
answer_key:
(a) Exhalation/Respiration - Lungs
(b) Egestion - Large Intestine/Anus
(c) Excretion - Kidneys
(d) Sweating (Perspiration) - Skin/Sweat glands
answer_explanation: Mapping waste items to excretory organs.

subject: Science
question_text: Paheli noticed water being pulled up by a motor-pump to an overhead tank of a five-storeyed building. She wondered how water moves up to great heights in the tall trees standing next to the building. Can you tell why?
answer_key: Water moves up to great heights in tall trees due to the transpiration pull. Water evaporating from leaves creates a suction force that pulls water upwards through the xylem vessels from the roots.
answer_explanation: Comparing mechanical pumps to biological systems.

subject: Science
question_text: Match the parts of the heart in Column I with the direction of flow of blood in Column II.
answer_key: Matching parts of the heart. Right ventricle -> Pushes blood into the pulmonary artery. Pulmonary veins -> Bring oxygenated blood from lungs. Left atrium -> Receives oxygenated blood from pulmonary veins. Pulmonary arteries -> Take deoxygenated blood to lungs. Left ventricle -> Pushes blood into the aorta. Right auricle -> Receives deoxygenated blood from the body.
answer_explanation: Tracing the flow of blood through the heart.
provenance_excerpt: Match the parts of the heart in Column I with the direction of flow of blood in Column II.

subject: Science
question_text:
Read the following terms given below.
root hairs, xylem, urethra, arteries, kidneys, veins, atria, capillaries, heart, ureter, phloem, urinary bladder
Group the terms on the basis of the categories given below.
(a) Circulatory system of animals.
(b) Excretory system in human.
(c) Transport of substances in plants.
answer_key:
(a) Circulatory: arteries, veins, atria, capillaries, heart
(b) Excretory: urethra, kidneys, ureter, urinary bladder
(c) Plants: root hairs, xylem, phloem
answer_explanation: Categorizing biological terms by system.

subject: Science
question_text:
Fill in the blanks of the following paragraph using just two words – arteries and veins.
___(a)___ carry oxygen-rich blood from the heart to all parts of the body and ___ (b)___ carry carbon dioxide-rich blood from all parts of the body back to the heart. ___ (c)___ have thin walls and __ (d)___ have thick elastic walls. Blood flows at high pressure in ___ (e)____. Valves are present in __ (f) ___ which allow blood to flow only towards the heart. __ (g)___ divide into smaller vessels. These vessels further divide into extremely thin tubes called capillaries. The capillaries join up to form ____ (h)_____.
answer_key:
(a) Arteries
(b) veins
(c) Veins
(d) arteries
(e) arteries
(f) veins
(g) Arteries
(h) veins
answer_explanation: Detailed knowledge of arteries and veins properties.

subject: Science
question_text:
While learning to ride a bicycle Boojho lost his balance and fell. He got bruises on his knees and it started bleeding. However, the bleeding stopped after some time.
(a) Why did the bleeding stop?
(b) What would be the colour of the wounded area and why?
(c) Which type of blood cells are responsible for clotting of blood?
answer_key:
(a) The bleeding stopped due to the process of blood clotting.
(b) The wounded area becomes dark red/brown due to the formation of a clot, which consists of a network of threads that traps red blood cells.
(c) Platelets are responsible for the clotting of blood.
answer_explanation: Applying blood clotting concepts to an injury.
provenance_excerpt: While learning to ride a bicycle Boojho lost his balance and fell. He got bruises on his knees and it started bleeding.
